must_have_breakdown.skill: Salesforce Platform Management or Salesforce Architecture
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Candidate manages Salesforce environments and sandbox management but no evidence of platform architecture design or Center of Excellence setup.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Expert-level proficiency with Copado for CI/CD and release orchestration
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Holds four Copado certifications (Fundamentals I & II in both SFP and Metadata Format) and has used Copado as a primary tool across two employers for 4+ years.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Deep understanding of Salesforce multi-tenant architecture
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Resume demonstrates practical environment management and sandbox handling but contains no explicit mention of multi-tenant architecture knowledge or governor limits.
Conducted environment refresh activities across Salesforce orgs, ensuring data consistency and release readiness, across two roles spanning 4+ years.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Communication skills to translate technical releases into business-friendly roadmaps
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Facilitated post-release reviews and stakeholder management are listed, but no evidence of translating technical releases into business roadmaps for senior leadership.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Experience managing Business Critical production environments
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Provided 24/7 production support and delivered critical production releases, but no explicit mention of 'Business Critical' tier environments or Salesforce premier support.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Working with Salesforce premier support tiers

must_have_breakdown.evidence: Holds Salesforce Platform Developer I certification and performed VS Code-based deployments, but deep hands-on development (Apex, triggers, SOQL) is not evidenced in job experience.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Custom objects, workflows, triggers, SOQL & SOSL

must_have_breakdown.evidence: Resume references enforcing 'release best practices and governance standards' but no evidence of Salesforce application design patterns or Force.com platform best practices.
Worked in a client-facing enterprise environment managing releases, though the resume describes collaborative team-based environments without explicit evidence of independent ownership.

reasoning: Nandhini is a strong Copado DevOps and Release Engineer with genuine, well-evidenced expertise in CI/CD pipeline management, environment refresh, and release governance using Copado in enterprise settings. However, this JD requires a Platform Manager operating at a senior architectural level — designing a Center of Excellence, federating development strategy, managing Salesforce multi-tenant architecture, and applying Apex/SOQL/triggers/design patterns. Nandhini's profile is primarily operational and tool-execution focused, not architectural or managerial in scope. Critical must-haves like governor limits, Salesforce premier support tiers, custom objects/SOQL/triggers development, and architectural design are entirely absent from the resume. With 4+ years of Salesforce experience versus the 5-year minimum required, and a role operating at mid-level rather than senior/lead, there is a meaningful gap between her current scope and the expectations of this role.
strengths: Expert-level Copado proficiency validated by four certifications (Fundamentals I & II in both SFP and Metadata Format) combined with 4+ years of hands-on deployment execution at two enterprise employers — directly addressing the JD's most critical must-have.

Demonstrated CI/CD pipeline setup and governance at Atos Syntel, including configuring Copado pipelines with Git-based Bitbucket version control and enforcing release governance standards across enterprise telecom environments.
Quantified delivery impact with a 30% reduction in deployment time through streamlined Copado processes, providing concrete evidence of operational improvement rather than just task completion.

Breadth of Copado deployment modes — handling both SFP and Metadata Format deployments, Agentforce AI component deployments, and Copado CLI operations — reflecting modern, current tooling that aligns with the JD's Agentforce and Data Cloud context.

Stakeholder and cross-functional experience including 24/7 production support, release risk assessments, post-release reviews, and mentoring junior team members, demonstrating the communication and leadership soft skills the role requires.
gaps: No evidence of Salesforce development depth (Apex, triggers, SOQL, SOSL, custom objects, design patterns) — this role requires hands-on development experience on the Force.com platform, not just deployment execution.

Governor limits and Salesforce multi-tenant architecture understanding are entirely absent from the resume, which are explicitly listed must-haves for a Platform Manager overseeing ecosystem health and scalability.

No experience mentioned with Salesforce premier support tiers or managing Business Critical production environments at the tier level the JD specifies, which is a key operational responsibility of the role.

Architectural and strategic responsibilities — such as designing a federated development strategy, architecting a Center of Excellence, or building org-wide governance frameworks — are not evidenced anywhere in the candidate's experience.

4+ years of Salesforce experience falls slightly below the 5-year minimum, and the candidate's seniority is mid-level rather than senior/manager, meaning she has not yet operated at the scope of ownership this Platform Manager role demands.

scoring_factors.reason: No notice period stated in the resume and JD specifies no maximum, so no penalty applied.
JD does not specify a work structure or location, so no location-based penalty applies.
Candidate has 4+ years of Salesforce experience, which exceeds half of the 5-year JD minimum threshold of 2.5 years.
JD gender preference is 'Any', so no penalty applies.
JD has no college preference or exclusion specified, so no adjustment applies.
